headline: Socceroos shock the dark horses: Australia 2-0 Türkiye, and a 20-year-old refugee writes history
summary: Australia stunned Euro 2024 quarter-finalists Türkiye 2-0 in their Group D opener at BC Place, Vancouver (June 14, 2026). Türkiye — tournament dark horses with Real Madrid's Arda Güler pulling the strings — had 71.7% possession and Güler took a WC-high 8 shots, but were repelled by keeper Patrick Beach (8 saves, record debut) and a deep Aussie block. Nestory Irankunda, 20, latched onto Okon-Engstler's long ball and finished on 27' to become the YOUNGEST Australian ever to score at a World Cup (20y 125d, beating Brett Holman by ~6 years); Connor Metcalfe sealed it from range on 75' after a midfield turnover. Yildiz came on at half-time but couldn't change it. A deserved upset that leaves Australia level with the USA atop Group D and Türkiye pointless.
angles: The upset: the dark horses humbled — Türkiye bossed the ball, Australia bossed the scoreboard (deep block + two clinical counters)
Irankunda's coming-of-age: 20-year-old born in Tanzania to Burundian parents, ex-refugee, cuts inside and finishes like Michael Owen vs Argentina '98 — youngest Socceroo to ever score at a World Cup
Group D shaped: USA top (beat Paraguay 4-1), Australia 2nd, Türkiye 3rd with 0 — the host's group has a new dark horse, and it isn't Türkiye
Yildiz rested, Türkiye misfired: leaving the Juventus jewel on the bench backfired — should he have started?
Popovic's plan: sit deep, absorb, punish — a blueprint underdog nations will copy at this World Cup
